sections.content:

- Clicking the yellow segment caused a board change. Need to see what changed exactly.
- The pink bar at the top may be a timer that shrinks with actions.
- Need to compare before/after to identify gameplay vs HUD changes.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>

# Compare before and after frames
before = last_transition.before_frame
after = last_transition.after_frame

before_seg = before.segmentation
after_seg = after.segmentation

print("=== BEFORE ===")
for n in before_seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] < 500:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)} cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)} hash={n['hash']}")

print("\n=== AFTER ===")
for n in after_seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] < 500:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)} cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)} hash={n['hash']}")

# Check what changed - compare node properties
print("\n=== DIFF ===")
before_hashes = {n['hash']: n for n in before_seg['nodes']}
after_hashes = {n['hash']: n for n in after_seg['nodes']}

# Find new or removed hashes
before_hash_set = set(before_hashes.keys())
after_hash_set = set(after_hashes.keys())

print("New hashes:", after_hash_set - before_hash_set)
print("Removed hashes:", before_hash_set - after_hash_set)

# Check for pixel count changes
for h in before_hash_set & after_hash_set:
    if before_hashes[h]['pixels'] != after_hashes[h]['pixels']:
        print(f"Hash {h}: pixels {before_hashes[h]['pixels']} -> {after_hashes[h]['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! The key change is:
- The pink bar (P) shrank from 64 pixels to 63 pixels, and the last cell (col 63) became charcoal (c).
- All other gameplay objects stayed the same (same hashes, same positions).
So the pink bar is indeed a timer/progress bar that shrinks with each action. The only change from clicking the yellow segment was the timer decreasing. No gameplay objects changed.
This means clicking the yellow segment didn't do anything meaningful to the puzzle - it just consumed a timer tick. I need to find what actually interacts with the puzzle elements.
Let me think about what to click next:
1. The blue blocks (4x4) at the right edge
2. The charcoal blob + yellow strip at the bottom
3. The black bar segments themselves
4. The yellow segment again (but different position?)
Since the yellow segment click didn't change anything gameplay-wise, let me try clicking on the blue blocks or the charcoal blob. Let me try the blue block first.
